# Learnings

- Product specs require habits to appear in alphabetical order by name. Persisted manual ordering is not part of the accepted UX, so `DisplayOrder` and reorder flows should not be reintroduced unless the specs change.
- Checkin records are presence-only: a `Checkins` row should exist only for completed days, and same-day uncheck should delete the row rather than persist a false state.
- Habit deletion is confirmed through a `MudDialog` on the Habit Details page, while the page itself owns the `IHabitService.DeleteAsync(...)` call, redirects to home on success, and relies on SQLite cascade delete to remove related `Checkins`.
- Habit editing now uses a separate `MudDialog` form from the Habit Details page; the dialog performs `IHabitService.UpdateAsync(...)`, then the page reloads in place so the user stays on Habit Details.
- The core unit test project now lives under `tests\Streak.Core.UnitTests` to mirror `src\Streak.Core`, and individual unit test files should group scenarios with `#region Positive tests`, `#region Negative tests`, and `#region Boundary tests` where applicable.
- Habit details now compose streak/history through `Components\Cards\HabitStreakHistoryCard.razor`, while `HabitDetails.razor` stays focused on loading/orchestration and `HabitHistoryHeatmapBuilder` in `Streak.Core` projects raw checkins into the heatmap view model.
- `MainLayout.razor` now supplies a custom MudBlazor theme so the app's semantic colors match the specs: `Secondary` is teal for history accents, `Success` is green for checked-in state, and muted helper text should use `--mud-palette-text-secondary` rather than `Color.Secondary`.
- View model types under `src\Streak.Core\Models\ViewModels\` should use `#region Display Properties`, `#region Computed Properties`, and `#region Hidden Properties` to separate UI-bound values from derived/internal ones; input models may use only the regions that actually apply.
- The deploy workflow should publish Android as an arm64-only APK using `-r android-arm64 -p:AndroidPackageFormat=apk`, then upload the generated `*-Signed.apk` as an artifact so the CI output stays sideloadable for personal devices without inflating the APK with emulator/x86 runtimes.
- The app-wide habit cap is centralized in `src\Streak.Core\Constants\CoreConstants.cs` as `CoreConstants.MaxHabitCount`; create-flow enforcement and homepage CTA visibility should use that shared constant instead of duplicating numbers.
- In Azure Functions isolated worker with ASP.NET Core integration, `IActionResult`, `HttpRequest`, and ASP.NET-style result types are supported, but ASP.NET Core routing is not; Function routes must still live on `[HttpTrigger(..., Route = ...)]`, even when endpoint classes derive from `NControllerBase`.
- Settings export is platform-specific by design: Windows should use a native `FileSavePicker` and treat dialog cancellation as a non-error, while Android should write the backup into `MediaStore.Downloads` instead of opening a share sheet.
- The current Settings UI only implements the backup/export card, not the reminder section yet; keep that card's copy concise with `Backup`, `Download DB`, and the short Windows/Android save note from the Settings mockup/spec.
